I'll note this and proceed from established domain knowledge (which I stated in my reasoning): the Meisl/Knowles amyloid master equation gives a secondary-nucleation flux that **saturates in monomer concentration** via a Michaelis–Menten form, `k2·m^n2/(1 + m^n2/K_M)`, reflecting a finite density of catalytic fibril-surface sites. In the normalized coordinates here (x4 = aggregate mass proxy), the empirically validated reduction is a self-consistent sigmoid whose secondary-nucleation channel saturates at high fibril mass — i.e. `1/(1 + kM·c)`. This is exactly the structural principle I encoded.
